revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Itate-jinja (Wakayama)]][[ja:伊達神社 (和歌山市)]] {{Expand Japanese|伊達神社 (和歌山市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Itate Shrine | native_name = 伊達神社 | image = Itate-jinja (Wakayama) shaden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Itate Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.264425|135.195903}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛神|simple|Isotakeru-no-kami|zh|五十猛神|qid=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Itate Shrine'''|伊達神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kii Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kii Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kii Province|ja|紀伊国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11604948|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kii Province}} ** Series ordinal: 20 ** P155: {{ill|Q135041279|ltq=Q135041279|qid=Q135041279|1=Takatsumihimeno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Shima Shrine|lt=Shima Shrine|ja|志磨神社|zh|志磨神社|qid=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q200747|ltq=Q200747|qid=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}} * {{ill|Q864053|ltq=Q864053|qid=Q864053|1=Kii Province}} * {{ill|Nagusa district|lt=Nagusa district|ja|名草郡|zh|名草郡|qid=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.264425|135.195903}} == image (P18) == * Itate-jinja (Wakayama) shaden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Isotakeru|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛神|simple|Isotakeru-no-kami|zh|五十猛神|qid=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20150606012022/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520102.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183153 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11123280|ltq=Q11123280|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071127|ltq=Q11071127|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11123338|ltq=Q11123338|qid=Q11123338|1=Senior Fourth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071123|ltq=Q11071123|qid=Q11071123|1=Junior Third Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8170005000566 == Commons category (P373) == * Itate-jinja (Wakayama) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '和歌山県和歌山市園部1580', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Itate-jinja (Wakayama)}} [[JA:伊達神社 (和歌山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11380847}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Date Shrine |Image = [[File:Itate-jinja (Wakayama) shaden.JPG|250px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|qid=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|qid=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}1580 Sonobe |Location = {{Coord|34|15|52.78|N|135|11|45.49|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Date Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Kamiyaomi no Mikoto|Q11588756|qid=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown
({{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}}11 years ago ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|qid=Q23835|1=844}})) |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Sonobe Shrine / Sonobe Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = Kisansho Shrine |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=October 13th|Q2925|qid=Q2925|1=October 13}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Date Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Itate-jinja (Wakayama) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Date Shrine'' (Itate Jinja/Date Jinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|qid=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|qid=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}} Sonobe. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.Another name is "Sonobe Shrine (Sonobe Shrine)". Also {{ill|lt=Shima Shrine|Q17212913|qid=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} (Wakayama City{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|qid=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}), {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q28693031|qid=Q28693031|1=Shizuhi Shrine}} (Wada, Wakayama City, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamayama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}Setsusha), it is called ``Kinosanshoshya'' {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. === Enshrined God === The enshrined deities are as follows.The description of the two enshrined deities is according to the shrine history book [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1012 Date Shrine] (Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency). However, in the "Meiji Shrine Journal", in addition to the two pillars, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} is listed as the Aiden deity. . :* '''{{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}''' (Itakeru no Mikoto) - {{ill|lt=Mikogami|Q352176|qid=Q352176|1=Mikogami}} of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Suenakison|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. The original deity. :* '''{{ill|lt=Kamiyaimimi no Mikoto|Q11588756|qid=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}''' (Kamuyaimimi no Mikoto) - Later enshrined.In order to know the character of the "Itate deity" worshiped at Date Shrine, "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|qid=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}" describes {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Empress Jingu|1=UNKNOWN}} is said to have enshrined ``Itayo no Kami'' in front of his ship when crossing to Korea {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=320-321}}. Furthermore, in ``{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindaiki|Q11381815|qid=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}'', Funatama Shrine, a Sumiyoshi Taisha Sessha, is said to be the deity of the Ki clan and the headquarters of ``Shima-kami, Shizuka-kami, and Date-kami'' {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=320-321}}. In this way, ``Itate-kami'' has a deep relationship with ships, and is said to be the god of the Ki clan who traveled to various places by ship {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=320-321}}. The personal deity Gojutake no Mikoto is a tree god symbolizing the ``Land of Trees'' (Ki Province), and since ships are made by cutting down trees, he was also considered the guardian deity of ships {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=320-321}}.In addition, since Gojutake no Mikoto is the same deity as the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|qid=Q11379271|1=Itakiso shrine}}, Date Shrine was also called ``Ichinomiya Daimyojin'' {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=89}}. One theory is that in ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|qid=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}'', ``Kisansho Shrine'' is said to enshrine the three deities of Idakeso (Itake no Mikoto, Oya Tsuhime no Mikoto, and Tomato Hime no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}However, the two pillars other than Isotake no Mikoto are highly questionable {{Harv|Date Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=320-321}}. . The enshrined deity, Yaimimi no Mikoto, is said to be the ancestor of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sonobe clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, who lived in this area in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, according to the ``Illustrated Guide to Famous Places of Kii Province'' {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. As mentioned later, the transfer to Sonobe was {{ill|lt=Joan (Japan)|Q1194188|qid=Q1194188|lt=Jōan|1=Jōan (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1174|Q19690|qid=Q19690|1=1174}}), and based on this theory, it is believed that Kami Yaimimi no Mikoto was enshrined after the transfer.{{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}} In addition to the two above-mentioned pillars, it is said that the father god of the 50 Takenomi no Mikoto, Suenmeison, was once enshrined as Gion {{ill|lt=Gozu Tenno|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}{{Sfn|Date Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=320-321}}. === History === [[File:Itate-jinja (Wakayama) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] === Founding === Founding date is unknown. {{jalink|Cheng'an (Japan)|Q1194188|According to an ancient document dated 4 years ago ({{ill|lt=1174|Q19690|qid=Q19690|1=1174}}), "Kisansho Shrine" including Date Shrine Since it appears to have been located near the shrine|Q888184|Naemiya}}, the transfer to its current location in Sonobe is said to have taken place after that time {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. === Outline === In national history, both are known as Shima-kami and Shizuka-kami.{{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} In 11 years ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|qid=Q23835|1=844}}), he was given the lower rank of Seigoi, and in {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}}3 years ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|qid=Q30364|1=850}}) he was given the rank of junior fourth rank and lower. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), he was promoted to Seishiyo (lower rank), in the 17th year of Jogan ({{jalink|875|Q23816}) }) Contains an article about the promotion of Junior Third Rank {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}{{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmōcho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmōcho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|qid=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|qid=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} is ``Date Shrine'' In addition to being listed as ``Meishin-dai'' and listed in {{ill|lt=Meishin-taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, in ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|qid=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}'' it is listed as ``Shoichii Date Okami'' {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}.Also, Date Shrine is {{ill|lt=Shima Shrine|Q17212913|qid=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} (Wakayama City {{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|qid=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}) and {{ill|lt=Shizuhi Shrine|Q28693031|qid=Q28693031|1=Shizuhi Shrine}} (Wada, Wakayama City), it is said to have been called ``Kinosanshoshya'' {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. The description of "Kisansho-sha" starts with the storage rice book of {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|qid=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}}3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|qid=Q19359|1=1048}}), and also in "{{ill|lt=middle right record|Q11363231|qid=Q11363231|1=Chūyūki}}" {{ill|lt=Tennin|Q1193599|qid=Q1193599|1=Tennin (era)}}2nd year ({{ill|lt=1109|Q19515|qid=Q19515|1=1109}}) article, ``{{ill|lt=Ryojin Hisho|Q3010584|qid=Q3010584|1=Ryōjin Hishō}}'' appears to be a Shiku divine poem {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. In "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|qid=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}", about the origin of Kisansho Shrine, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sankan Conquest|1=UNKNOWN}} is said to have been enshrined to {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}.Our company has been compared to the above-mentioned ``Date Shrine,'' but according to ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|qid=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}},'' there was a debate over this categorization in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}{{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. In other words, even though our company had previously called itself Date Shrine, during the {{ill|lt=Kanpo|Q1145798|qid=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} years ({{ill|lt=1741|Q7573|qid=Q7573|1=1741}}-{{ill|lt=1744|Q7583|qid=Q7583|1=1744}}) {{ill|lt=Suimon Fukiage Shrine|Q11549496|qid=Q11549496|1=Minato-Fukiage Shrine}} in Ono-cho, Yama City claimed to be Shikinai Date Shrine, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayama Domain|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized this claim {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. Therefore, in "Ki Meisho Zue", Fukiage Shrine is compared to Date Shrine, and we called it "Sonobe Shrine" {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. Also, in "Zoku Fudoki", it is said that our shrine was burnt down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, but the truth is unclear {{Sfn|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=437-438}}. Entered {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} and changed the company name back to its old name, ``Date Shrine.'' In the 14th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} November 3, 11 years ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|qid=Q23835|1=844}}), from Jugoinoge to Shogoikege ('{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}') - Written as 'Date God'. ** October 21, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} ({{ill|lt=850 years|Q30364|qid=Q30364|1=850}}), Junior Fourth Rank Lower (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor Annals|Q1052905|qid=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Date God.'' ** {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower rank of the fourth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Date God.'' ** October 17, 17th year of the Jogan era ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|qid=Q23816|1=875}}), from the top of the fourth rank to the third rank of junior rank (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Date God.'' * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|qid=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}'') - Written as ``Date Okami''. === Setsusha === [[File:Itate-jinja (Wakayama) keidaisha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] Both precinct shrines Setsumetsu shrine festivals are from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1012 Date Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). . '''Suesha''' * Amaterasu Omikami, Kinmine Shrine, Hachioji Shrine * Inari Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Camphor Shrine * Kasuga Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Awashima Shrine * Hayao Shrine, Tenpanza Shrine '''Small shrine''' * Otona Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Akazai Shrine * Tsune Shrine, Myoken Shrine, Otona Shrine * Tenma Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Ebisu Shrine === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|qid=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|qid=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}} Sonobe 1580 '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West Japan) {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|qid=Q1154800|1=Hanwa Line}} {{ill|lt=Rokujutani Station|Q857433|qid=Q857433|1=Musota Station}} (about 18 minutes walk) * Bus: {{ill|lt=Wakayama Bus|Q11417290|qid=Q11417290|1=Wakayama Bus}} Rokujuya Line 83/84 Ukou stop (about 5 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Ukou - A place name in the eastern part of Sonobe, Wakayama City. The name ``Isao'' is said to be derived from ``Isao no Kami'', another name for Isao no Mikoto. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{reflist|group="Note"}} === Source === {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Date Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/219}} "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Part 2"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection) Reference frames 219-220. * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Hiroyoshi Niu|chapter=Date Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakukan University|Q1797584|qid=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Date Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 31 Place names of Wakayama prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref=}}** {{Wikicite|reference="Date Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Date Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Shima Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}. * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Kentori Maruyama|year=1986|chapter=Date Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 6 Ise/Shima/Iga/Kii|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|Date Shrine (Gods)|1986}}}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Itate-jinja
